instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
Is a Term Insurance Plan Portable?
Meta description: Want to know all about term life insurance portability? Check out this article to understand what it is and explore the benefits it offers.
Keywords: term life insurance, term insurance benefits, what is term plan
Life can be extremely unpredictable at times. When you least expect it, an unforeseen event may occur and sideline your financial plans. This may leave the dependent members of your family without any financial support. However, life insurance can help you with this. More specifically, term life insurance, which is the most affordable kind of life insurance, can give you a significant life cover for a nominal premium.
What is a term plan?
A term plan is a kind of life insurance policy that offers only a pure life cover. This cover is valid for a particular term, known as the policy term. This means that in case the policyholder passes away during the policy term, the life insurance company will pay out death benefits to the nominee mentioned in the policy.
In order to avail this life cover, policyholders need to pay a premium to the life insurance provider as per the terms and conditions of the plan. This premium may be paid as a one-time payment in single premium policies, or as periodic payments in regular or limited premium policies.
There are many term insurance benefits that you receive when you buy this kind of life insurance. However, sometimes, you may find that your life insurance provider has some conditions that you may find unfavorable. Or, for any other reason, you may not be satisfied with your term life insurance provider. In such a case, can you make use of term plan portability? Let’s find out.
What is term plan portability?
Term plan portability is a theoretical concept where the policyholder is allowed to transfer their policy from one insurance provider to another. So, all the risks associated with that policy will be taken on by the new insurance provider. This concept is in practice in other kinds of insurance like health insurance. However, in the case of term insurance, which is a life insurance, the practice of portability is not available yet.
However, the IRDAI is considering the proposal of allowing term plan policyholders to port their policy if needed. If it is implemented, this practice will promote a fair market and encourage insurers to ensure that their existing customers are satisfied with their products and policies.
It also brings up the question of transfer of term insurance benefits. Will your current benefits get transferred when you port your policy? The answer to that will become clearer once the IRDAI lays the ground rules for term plan portability.
Benefits of term plan portability
If IRDAI does make term plan portability possible, it can have many benefits for policyholders. Here are a few of them.
Continued coverage for the policyholder
In the current scenario, if a policyholder is not satisfied with an insurer, they may have to purchase a new term plan from a different insurance provider. During the intermittent period between surrendering the old policy and buying a new one, the person may be unprotected. But allowing people to port their term plans can ensure continued and uninterrupted coverage for the policyholder without compromising any of the term insurance benefits.
Improves customer satisfaction
Term insurance portability also improves customer satisfaction greatly. It encourages insurers to improve their service levels and focus more on customer retention. So, customer service and support will see a universal boost. Furthermore, it also gives customers a wider variety of choice when they wish to purchase a term plan, since it will be possible to port the plan later if they are not satisfied with the insurer.
Reduces lapse rate on term insurance plans
Many people tend to stop paying term plan premiums and let their policy lapse if they are not happy with the insurance provider they chose initially. This increases the lapse rate. However, portability may help solve this issue because in case a policyholder is dissatisfied, they can simply port their term plan to another insurer instead of letting the cover lapse.
Conclusion
Talks for term insurance portability are only its early stages now. Once the insurance regulator clarifies their stance on this practice, there will be greater clarity on how portability of term insurance and life covers in general will work out. The challenge lies in the fact that unlike many other general insurance policies, life insurance is a long-term product. However, if implemented, it could have many benefits for policyholders, as you saw above.
https://www.canarahsbclife.com/blog/term-insurance/is-a-term-insurance-policy-portable.html
